2604.23954.title: An empirical evaluation of the risks of AI model updates using clinical data: stability, arbitrariness, and fairness
2604.23954.paper_summary: Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AI/ML) models used in clinical settings are increasingly deployed to support clinical decision-making. However, when training data become stale due to changes in demographics, environment, or patient behaviors, model performance can degrade substantially. While updating models with new training data is necessary, such updates may also introduce new risks. We evaluated the proposed monitoring framework on four publicly available U.S.-based Type 1 Diabetes datasets containing high-resolution continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data, comprising approximately 11,300 weekly observations from 496 participants under 20 years of age. All datasets included structured sociodemographic information. Using the prediction of severe hyperglycemia events in children with type 1 diabetes as a case study, we examine how different model update strategies can adversely affect model stability (e.g., by causing predictions to "flip" for a large number of cases after an update), increase arbitrariness in predictions, or worsen accuracy equity and the balance of error rates across subpopulations. We propose multiple dimensions for continuous monitoring to detect these issues and argue that such monitoring is essential for the development of trustworthy clinical decision support systems.
2604.23954.paper_summary_zh: 人工智慧與機器學習（AI/ML）模型在臨床環境中的應用越來越多，以支持臨床決策。<lb/>然而，當訓練數據因人口統計、環境或病人行為的變化而變得過時時，模型性能可能會顯著下降。<lb/>雖然使用新的訓練數據來更新模型是必要的，但這樣的更新也可能引入新的風險。<lb/>我們在四個公開可用的美國1型糖尿病數據集上評估了所提出的監測框架，這些數據集包含高解析度的連續血糖監測（CGM）數據，總計約11,300個來自496名20歲以下參與者的每週觀察。<lb/>所有數據集均包含結構化的社會人口信息。<lb/>以預測1型糖尿病兒童的嚴重高血糖事件為案例研究，我們檢視不同的模型更新策略如何對模型穩定性產生不利影響（例如，在更新後導致大量案例的預測“翻轉”）、增加預測的任意性，或惡化準確性公平性及各子群體之間的錯誤率平衡。<lb/>我們提出多個維度以進行持續監測，以檢測這些問題，並主張這種監測對於開發可信的臨床決策支持系統至關重要。
2604.23954.author: Ioannis Bilionis et.al.
2604.23954.authors: Ioannis Bilionis, Ricardo C. Berrios, Luis Fernandez-Luque, Carlos Castillo
